ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken curry entry (FOODON_00002809) but no specific code for the Singaporean kopi-shop curry chicken with French breadstick set meal format. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKM covers chicken meat dishes broadly; no specific code exists for Singaporean curry chicken breadstick set. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this specific Singaporean set meal combination.

serving.description: 1 set meal (curry chicken with soft French breadstick, M-size hot beverage, teacake)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal comprising approximately 200 g curry chicken with sauce, one soft French breadstick (~100 g), one M-size hot beverage (~240 ml), and one teacake (~80 g), totalling approximately 550 g solid and liquid components.

nutrient_source.title: Curry Chicken (HPB Singapore Foodo) combined with French bread and teacake component estimates from HPB Foodo and USDA SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Per-100g values are derived by weighted composition of three sub-components: (1) Singaporean curry chicken (HPB Foodo item 'Curry Chicken'), (2) French baguette (USDA SR Legacy item 18033 'Bread, French or Vienna'), and (3) teacake (USDA SR Legacy item 18350 'Cake, yellow, commercially prepared'). The hot beverage (kopi/teh) contributes negligible macros at the per-100g level of the solid meal and is excluded from the per-100g calculation. Weighted proportions: curry chicken 36%, breadstick 18%, teacake 15%, beverage 31% by mass. Per-100g values represent the blended solid-and-liquid composite.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for curry chicken and white bread
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific set meal. GI estimated as weighted composite: curry chicken component GI ~45 (low; University of Sydney GI Database, analogous South Asian chicken curry), French baguette GI ~73 (high; University of Sydney GI Database, white baguette), teacake GI ~63 (medium; University of Sydney GI Database, sponge cake analogue), hot beverage GI ~1 (negligible carbohydrate). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution: baguette dominates (~55% of carbs), curry chicken (~25%), teacake (~20%). Composite GI ≈ 52 (low-medium boundary, rounded to low). GL per serving = GI/100 × carbohydrate_g_per_serving = 52/100 × 79.75 g carbs in 550 g serving ≈ 41.5, rounded to 41.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~1705 mg per full set meal) from curry sauce, processed bread, and teacake
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this set meal to occasional consumption and request reduced-salt curry sauce, as a single serving may provide approximately 74% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~52) driven by refined white baguette and teacake; moderate carbohydrate load (~80 g per set meal)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious of the refined carbohydrate content from the French breadstick and teacake, which can elevate postprandial blood glucose; substituting a wholegrain bread option and omitting the teacake would reduce the glycemic load substantially.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk in curry sauce (~3.1 g per 100 g; ~17 g per set meal) and moderate cholesterol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit frequency of this meal due to the saturated fat contribution from coconut-milk-based curry, and should choose a smaller portion of curry sauce to reduce saturated fat intake below 10% of daily energy.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Curry Chicken with Soft French Breadstick Set is a popular Singaporean kopi-shop (coffee shop) set meal offered by Toast Box, a well-known local café chain. The set comprises a serving of Singaporean-style curry chicken — bone-in or boneless chicken pieces braised in a rich, aromatic curry sauce made with coconut milk, lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, chilli, and a blend of South and Southeast Asian spices — accompanied by a soft French baguette (breadstick) for dipping, an M-size hot beverage (typically kopi, kopi-o, teh, or teh-o), and a teacake. The curry sauce is characteristically creamy, mildly spicy, and fragrant, reflecting the Peranakan and Malay culinary influences prevalent in Singaporean hawker and café culture. The soft French breadstick serves as the primary carbohydrate vehicle, intended to be torn and dipped into the curry sauce, a practice common in Singaporean breakfast and brunch culture. Nutritionally, the set meal is a moderate-energy, moderate-protein meal with notable sodium content from the curry sauce and processed bread. The saturated fat contribution from coconut milk warrants attention for cardiovascular health. The glycemic index of the composite meal is estimated at approximately 52 (low-medium), driven primarily by the refined white baguette. Clinically, the meal is of moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to refined carbohydrate content, and of high concern for hypertension due to sodium load. It is suitable for halal diets and is a common breakfast or lunch option in Singapore's café dining scene.

confidence_reason: No single approved source provides exact nutrient data for this specific set meal combination. Per-100g values are derived from a weighted composite of HPB Foodo (curry chicken component) and USDA SR Legacy (French baguette and teacake components). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for component foods. Confidence is medium rather than low because all component sources are approved primary sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. 4. The set meal includes an M-size hot beverage (kopi/teh); the beverage contributes negligible macronutrients at the per-100g composite level and its GI contribution is treated as 1 (effectively zero carbohydrate). 5. Serving size of 550 g is an estimate based on typical Toast Box set meal portion weights; actual weights may vary by outlet. 6. Halal status is based on Toast Box's publicly stated halal certification in Singapore. 7. The Hindi alias entry 'करी चिकन और सॉफ्ट फ्रेंच ब्रेडस्टिक सेट' uses standard Devanagari script throughout. 8. GI composite estimate methodology: weighted by carbohydrate contribution of each component per University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries.
